title: fix: route Opus 4.7/4.8 via us.* Bedrock profile
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- `claude-opus-4.7`/`4.8` were failing nearly every request (monitor showed them "Off")
- Root cause: the `global.anthropic.claude-opus-4-7`/`4-8` Bedrock inference profiles return `InternalServerException`/`ServiceUnavailableException` at invoke time
- The `us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-7`/`4-8` profiles are healthy — verified directly against the Bedrock runtime (both return real responses; `global.*` both fail)
- Switches both models to the `us.*` profile; `opus-4-6` left on `global.*` (still works)
- Note: the inflated 4xx in the monitor is separate — 402 (out-of-pollen spore users) + 401 (anon), expected traffic

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Opus finds a new path
gist.blurb: Claude Opus 4.7 and 4.8 were hitting some turbulence, so we rerouted them to healthier servers. Your heavy-duty text generation is back to buzzing smoothly.
gist.summary: Switched Claude Opus 4.7 and 4.8 to use the us.* AWS Bedrock inference profiles instead of the failing global.* profiles.
gist.impact: Requests to claude-opus-4.7 and claude-opus-4.8 will now succeed reliably instead of returning internal server errors.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a network router/traffic controller, switching glowing data streams from a broken path to a healthy green path. Nomnom creature eating the fixed data.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny traffic controller hat, rerouting glowing data streams on a retro terminal map. She switches a broken path to a healthy lime green (#ecf874) path. The round Nomnom creature sits nearby, happily eating the fresh data. Soft pastel gradient background, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
